name: multi-image with contradicting observation - lowered confidence
raw: {"thought":"The first image shows the geyser's pressure relief valve actively dripping, with white scale build-up on the tundish below — primary evidence of a failing PRV. The second image, however, shows the electrical isolator box adjacent to the geyser with visible scorching around the cable gland, which points instead to an electrical fault on the supply side. These two findings are not mutually consistent: a PRV failure is a plumbing issue, while scorched cable entry is electrical. I have anchored the diagnosis to the directly observed dripping PRV (primary evidence) and flagged the scorching as a contradicting signal that needs separate attention.","diagnosis":"Failing Pressure Relief Valve","message":"The geyser's pressure relief valve is dripping consistently into the tundish, which is a clear sign that the valve seal has failed or that the system pressure is exceeding the valve's rated limit. Replacing the valve and confirming the static pressure is the first step.\n\nThe scorching visible on the electrical isolator box in the second image is a separate concern and should be investigated before the geyser is re-energised.","action_required":"","contractor_checklist":["Isolate water and power to the geyser before any work begins.","Replace the pressure relief valve with a manufacturer-matched unit.","Test static and dynamic supply pressure; install a PRV at the meter if pressure exceeds 400 kPa.","Inspect the scorched cable gland and replace the cable termination if compromised."],"homeowner_prep":"Switch off the geyser breaker at the DB board until the contractor arrives.","image_descriptions":["Pressure relief valve actively dripping into a tundish below, with white limescale build-up on the rim.","Electrical isolator box mounted next to geyser, with visible scorching and discoloration around the lower cable entry gland."],"image_observations":[{"primary_observation":"Pressure relief valve dripping consistently into the tundish, with limescale build-up.","components_visible":["pressure relief valve","tundish","copper drain pipe"],"components_missing_or_damaged":["pressure relief valve (leaking, scaled)"],"role_in_diagnosis":"primary_evidence"},{"primary_observation":"Electrical isolator box with scorching around the lower cable gland.","components_visible":["isolator box","cable gland","supply cable"],"components_missing_or_damaged":["cable gland (scorched, discoloured)"],"role_in_diagnosis":"contradicting"}],"clarification_questions":[],"diy_verification":"Place a dry cup under the PRV outlet overnight; if water collects, the valve is failing.","photo_request":"","confidence_drivers":["Direct view of dripping PRV","Scorching introduces electrical concern","Lowered confidence due to multi-cause evidence"]}
